text.title: Chain of Thought — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `chain-of-thought` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: CoT is NEVER shown to the end-user. Strip the reasoning before returning the response.
rule.rationale: Leaked CoT confuses users, exposes system prompt, and creates injection surfaces.

rule.statement: Extended-thinking budget is bounded (max_thinking_tokens). Unbounded thinking is rejected.
rule.rationale: Unbounded thinking blows latency budgets and can produce minutes-long responses.

rule.statement: For high-stakes answers (numeric, factual), sample N=3-5 chains and majority-vote.
rule.rationale: Self-consistency adds cost but reduces single-chain hallucination ~25%.

rule.statement: On numeric / structured tasks, validate the CoT-produced answer against a deterministic check before returning.
rule.rationale: CoT can produce a confident wrong answer; cheap validators catch it.

rule.statement: CoT prompts are version-pinned in the repo (no inline string literals scattered).
rule.rationale: Prompt changes silently shift behaviour; pinning makes diffs reviewable.
